title: Ventral midbrain dopaminergic (mDA) Neuron Differentiation Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"375md","text":"Generating authentic functional  ventral midbrain dopaminergic neurons, those most vulnerable in Parkinson's disease, from human induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) has enabled cell replacement therapy clinical trials and increasingly sophisticated in vitro disease models. This protocol features further optimizations on a recently updated developmental patterning approach (Kim et al., 2021), focusing on optimizing key stages of t including cell maintenance, induction, cryopreservation, reassembly, and maturation. This comprehensive protocol details the  conditions required for the development of midbrain cell types including caudal ventral midbrain dopaminergic neurons and other resident neural cell types, including morphogens, media, substrates, timing, and enables selective enrichment of DA neurons as well as other regional cell types, and integrations with  organoid and assembloid cultures.   Importantly, variability between different cell line genetic backgrounds, and isogenic pairs, is reduced by by reducing the  impact of non-disease-related varitation in iPSC line-intrisic phenotypic variablkes like plating and repassaging efifciency at multiple bottlenecks,and leverages strategies for further inter-line normalization.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"m8jl","text":"Day -X: PSC expansion  ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dt062","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"erls6","text":"iPSC are cultured following Croft lab Protocols. It should have perfectly undifferentiated appearance and high viability.  Almost all other substrates and media are fine as well, with some caveats (Eb, see discussion) .https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mgfOIq4uSawHrjZtxbICilmuu64zIxRlk6SAWFPGm3Y/edit","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":219,"length":84}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":219,"length":84}],"data":{}},{"key":"di7ba","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5s5d1","text":"Day -2/-1 Form standardized pluripotent 3D aggregates (embryoid bodies); see Aggrewell Plates protocol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":77,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":77,"length":16}],"data":{}},{"key":"b46df","text":"\tSeed in Stemfleex media + RI or CEPT, 2000 cells/microwell (2.4M cells per well of 400/800 aggrewell 24wp wells)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7q8es","text":"\tNote: smooth spherical EBs should have formed the next day.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"296o1","text":"\tNote: EBs can be formed 1 or 2 days before differentiation.  Increase cells/microwell if using day -1.  Day 2 is standard","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6tedk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3qgq9","text":"Transfer EBs to ULA plates ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6i12o","text":"ULA 6wp, 10cm non-tc-treated dishes can be used based on user preference","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4igha","text":"All culture plates are kept on a shaker at 80 rpm (after release for  2 days )","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"p2vu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fhcg1","text":"CHIR Dose:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4oqqs","text":"CHIR99021 concentration may have to be altered to obtain the highest mDA neuron differentiation efficiency for different iPSC / ESC cell lines due to line-intrinsic differences in Wnt signaling tone and interacting signalling pathways,","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2mq6u","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1tga8","text":"Day 1 and Day 3 = CHIR99021 range of 0.7 μM to 2 μM; Standard is 1 μM\nDay 4, Day 6, and Day 9 = CHIR99021 range of 5-6 μM, Standard is 6 μM","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"utbi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"5858B51B64C211EF8AD50A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mgfOIq4uSawHrjZtxbICilmuu64zIxRlk6SAWFPGm3Y/edit"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"5858B51B64C211EF8AD50A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://docs.google.com/document/d/13kLLOmWhqrXp0iA1BcpMs681k84OmxZDgcRkAMXIPL8/edit"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"jfdk","text":"CHIR99021 concentrations above 7.5 μM results in dose-dependent cell death ( Kim et al., 2021)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: PSC cultures should be completely undifferentiated
Form EB aggregates (Day -2): iPSC are first nucleated and generated into embryoid bodies. Aggrewells, iPSC medium + CEPT (or Y27632) using the following protocol EB protocols.io
Neural induction and patterning (Day 0-11), CHIR boost 4-10

Static or shaking suspension culture (80 RPM) in 6-well plates or 10 cm plate . in Neurobasal media. Components Concentration 1 Neurobasal 2 N2 10.0% 3 B27 without retinoic acid (RA) 20.0% 4 SB-431542 10 μM 5 LDN193189 100 nM 6 Purmorphamine 1 μM 7 SAG 1 μM 8 NEAA 10% 9 Albumax 1% 10 CHIR-99021 1 μM Media Day 4-6 in Neurobasal media.

Followed by CHIR BOOST from Day 4-6 Component Concentration 1 Neurobasal 2 N2 10% 3 B27 without retinoic acid (RA) 20% 4 SB-431542 10 μM 5 LDN193189 100 nM 6 Purmorphamine 1 μM 7 SAG 1 μM 8 NEAA 10% 9 Albumax 1% 10 CHIR99021 6 μM Media day 4-6
Remove SB/LDN+SAG/PURO on Day 6 Component Concentration 1 Neurobasal 2 N2 10% 3 B27 without retinoic acid (RA) 20% 4 CHIR99021 3 μM 5 Albumax 1% Media day 7-9

Add trophic factors from Day 10 Component Concentration 1 Neurobasal 2 N2 10% 3 B27 without retinoic acid (RA) 20% 4 CHIR99021 3 μM 5 BDNF 20 ng/mL 6 GDNF 20 ng/mL 7 Ascorbic Acid 0.2 mM 8 TGFb3 1 ng/mL 9 dbCAMP 0.2 mM 10 Albumax 1% Media 10-11
Remove CHIR followed by Expansion and trophic support (Day 12-33) %s %s
Cultures can be harvested and cryopreserved or reconstituted at (Day 12-33)

Further maturation and extended Culture can be performed in the following ways Reseed as Mono-, co- or tri-culture (see survival assay protocol.io versions) Organoids / Assembloids (See Assembloid protocol)
see Neural dissociation protocol .io version, See Worthington protocol.io version, See crypreservation protocol.io version,
